# SOC Prime Documentation — SEO & Content Capabilities Analysis

This document analyzes whether the current Docusaurus project meets the requested SEO and content-management requirements, and provides step-by-step instructions for each capability.

**Project stack:** Docusaurus 3.10.1 (classic preset), React 19, MDX content, GitHub Pages deployment.

**Current deployment:** `https://sleepycapibara.github.io/Docu-Docu/` (see [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js)).

**Content structure:** Docs-only (`blog: false`), three autogenerated sidebars (Product Docs, FAQs, Release Notes), single locale (`en`).

---

## Quick reference

| # | Requirement | Verdict | Effort |
|---|-------------|---------|--------|
| 1 | Close/open pages for indexation | **Yes** | Low |
| 2 | Meta title / meta description | **Yes** | Low |
| 3 | Add/change H2–H4 | **Yes** | Low |
| 4 | Add/change author | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 5 | Add/change publication date | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 6 | Add/change table of contents | **Yes** | Low |
| 7 | Add/change related articles | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 8 | Add/change language versions | **Yes (with setup)** | High |
| 9 | Subdomain or main-domain hosting | **Yes (with setup)** | High |
| 10 | Schemas (FAQ, Author, page type) | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 11 | URL structure optimization | **Yes** | Low–Medium |
| 12 | Internal linking | **Yes** | Low |
| 13 | Images / screenshots in pages | **Yes** | Low |
| 14 | Breadcrumbs | **Yes** | Low |
| 15 | Image alts / descriptions | **Yes** | Low |
| 16 | Social media banner per article | **Yes** | Low |
| 17 | Categories | **Yes** | Low |
| 18 | Tags | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 19 | Visual changes on hub pages | **Yes (with setup)** | Medium |
| 20 | Analytics | **Yes (with setup)** | Low–Medium |

**Summary:** All 20 requirements are achievable in Docusaurus. None are blocked by the platform. Several are not yet configured in this project but can be added with frontmatter, plugins, or small custom components.

---

## Must have

### 1. Ability to close/open pages for indexation

**Verdict:** Yes — fully supported.

**Current state:**
- No pages currently use `draft`, `unlisted`, or `noindex` controls.
- Docusaurus automatically generates `sitemap.xml` at build time (via `@docusaurus/plugin-sitemap`, included in the classic preset).
- No custom `robots.txt` exists in `static/`.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — Hide a page from production (draft)

Add to the MDX frontmatter:

```yaml
---
draft: true
---
```

- In **development** (`npm start`): page is visible.
- In **production** (`npm run build`): page is excluded from the build entirely (not published, not in sitemap).

Use for: work-in-progress articles.

#### Option B — Publish but exclude from navigation and sitemap (unlisted)

```yaml
---
unlisted: true
---
```

- Page is accessible via direct URL.
- Excluded from sidebar, category index pages, and previous/next navigation.
- Still included in sitemap by default — combine with Option C if you need `noindex`.

Use for: pages you want linkable but not discoverable via nav.

#### Option C — Tell search engines not to index a page (noindex)

Add inside the MDX file (after frontmatter):

```mdx
<head>
  <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow" />
</head>
```

Or use only `noindex` if you still want link equity to flow:

```mdx
<head>
  <meta name="robots" content="noindex" />
</head>
```

#### Option D — Site-wide robots control

Create [`static/robots.txt`](static/robots.txt):

```txt
User-agent: *
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://help.socprime.com/sitemap.xml
```

Update the `Sitemap` URL when the production domain is finalized.

#### Option E — Exclude paths from sitemap

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js), configure the sitemap plugin:

```js
plugins: [
  [
    '@docusaurus/plugin-sitemap',
    {
      ignorePatterns: ['/docs/tutorial-basics/**'],
      filename: 'sitemap.xml',
    },
  ],
],
```

**Recommended combination for "closed" pages:**
- Not ready at all → `draft: true`
- Published but hidden from nav → `unlisted: true` + `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">`
- Fully open → no special flags (default)

---

### 2. Ability to add/change meta title / meta description

**Verdict:** Yes — fully supported via frontmatter.

**Current state:**
- Pages use the first `# H1` heading as the browser tab title.
- No `description` frontmatter is set on imported articles (e.g. [`docs/faq/email-notifications.mdx`](docs/faq/email-notifications.mdx)).
- The homepage sets description via `<Layout description="...">` in [`src/pages/index.js`](src/pages/index.js).

**How to implement:**

Add to any MDX file's frontmatter:

```yaml
---
title: Set Your Email Notifications on SOC Prime
description: Learn how to personalize email notification frequency, search profiles, content lists, and Lucene queries in SOC Prime.
---
```

| Field | Effect |
|-------|--------|
| `title` | `<title>` tag, Open Graph `og:title`, sidebar label (unless `sidebar_label` is set) |
| `description` | `<meta name="description">`, Open Graph `og:description` |

**Optional — custom sidebar label:**

```yaml
sidebar_label: Email Notifications
```

**Optional — keywords (not used by Google for ranking, but harmless):**

```yaml
keywords: [email, notifications, SOC Prime, alerts]
```

**Site-wide default** is set in [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):

```js
title: 'SOC Prime Documentation',
tagline: 'Detection intelligence turbocharged with AI — product guides, FAQs, and release notes.',
```

Per-page `title` overrides this for that page's `<title>` tag.

**Action for existing articles:** Add `title` and `description` to frontmatter for every imported MDX file. The import script ([`scripts/import-help-articles.js`](scripts/import-help-articles.js)) can be extended to write these fields automatically from help-center metadata.

---

### 3. Ability to add/change H2–H4

**Verdict:** Yes — standard Markdown/MDX.

**Current state:**
- Articles already use `##`, `###`, etc. (e.g. `## Turning on/off Notifications` in email-notifications).
- Docusaurus auto-generates heading anchors (slugified IDs) for deep linking.

**How to implement:**

```mdx
## Section (H2)

### Subsection (H3)

#### Detail (H4)
```

**Tips:**
- Only one `# H1` per page (the page title). Use `##` and below for content structure.
- To customize a heading's anchor ID:

```mdx
## Custom Section Name {#custom-anchor-id}
```

- To assign stable IDs across edits, run:

```bash
npm run write-heading-ids
```

This writes `<!--` comments with suggested IDs into your MDX files.

**SEO note:** H2–H4 structure feeds the auto-generated table of contents and helps search engines understand page hierarchy.

---

### 4. Ability to add/change author of page

**Verdict:** Yes — requires setup (no single built-in "author" field for docs).

**Current state:**
- Authors are hardcoded as italic text in the body, e.g. `*Written by Eugene*` in [`docs/faq/email-notifications.mdx`](docs/faq/email-notifications.mdx).
- This is **not** structured metadata — search engines and schema markup cannot read it reliably.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — `last_update` frontmatter (quickest)

```yaml
---
last_update:
  date: 2023-02-02
  author: Eugene
---
```

Docusaurus displays "Last updated on ... by ..." at the bottom of the doc page when `showLastUpdateAuthor` and `showLastUpdateTime` are enabled in config.

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js), add under `presets > classic > docs`:

```js
docs: {
  showLastUpdateAuthor: true,
  showLastUpdateTime: true,
  // ...
},
```

**Limitation:** `last_update` reflects the *last edit*, not necessarily the original author. For multi-author or "written by" vs "updated by" semantics, use Option B.

#### Option B — Custom frontmatter + display component (recommended)

1. Add custom fields to frontmatter:

```yaml
---
authors:
  - Eugene
original_author: Eugene
---
```

2. Create a React component `src/components/DocAuthors/index.js`:

```jsx
import React from 'react';

export default function DocAuthors({authors, date}) {
  if (!authors?.length) return null;
  const label = authors.length === 1 ? authors[0] : authors.join(', ');
  return (
    <p className="doc-authors">
      <em>Written by {label}</em>
      {date && <> · <em>{date}</em></>}
    </p>
  );
}
```

3. Use in MDX:

```mdx
import DocAuthors from '@site/src/components/DocAuthors';

<DocAuthors authors={["Eugene"]} date="February 2, 2023" />
```

4. Optionally register as a global MDX component in `src/theme/MDXComponents.js` so authors don't need per-file imports.

#### Option C — Centralized author profiles (for Author schema)

Create `authors.yml` at the project root (blog-style, works with docs too):

```yaml
eugene:
  name: Eugene
  title: Technical Writer
  url: https://socprime.com
  image_url: /img/authors/eugene.jpg
```

Reference in frontmatter:

```yaml
authors: [eugene]
```

**Migration task:** Replace all `*Written by ...*` body lines with structured frontmatter + component.

---

### 5. Ability to add/change date of publication

**Verdict:** Yes — requires setup.

**Current state:**
- Dates are hardcoded as italic text, e.g. `*February 2, 2023*`, `*May 18, 2026*`.
- No structured date frontmatter on imported articles.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — `last_update` (last modified date)

```yaml
---
last_update:
  date: 2023-02-02
  author: Eugene
---
```

Enable display in config (see Requirement 4).

#### Option B — Custom `date` frontmatter (original publication date)

Docusaurus docs do not have a built-in `date` field (that is a blog feature). Use a custom field:

```yaml
---
date: 2023-02-02
---
```

Display it with a component (same pattern as authors):

```mdx
import DocMeta from '@site/src/components/DocMeta';

<DocMeta date="2023-02-02" author="Eugene" />
```

#### Option C — Enable the blog plugin for release notes

If release notes should behave like dated posts, move them to `blog/` with `date` frontmatter. This is a larger structural change and is usually **not** recommended if you want release notes in the docs sidebar.

**Recommendation:** Use `date` for original publication and `last_update` for most recent edit. Update the import script to extract dates from help-center metadata instead of embedding them as italic text.

---

### 6. Ability to add/change table of contents

**Verdict:** Yes — automatic by default, configurable per page.

**Current state:**
- Docusaurus renders a right-sidebar TOC for all docs pages based on headings.
- No per-page TOC customization is configured yet.

**How to implement:**

#### Per-page — control which heading levels appear in TOC

```yaml
---
toc_min_heading_level: 2
toc_max_heading_level: 4
---
```

This shows H2–H4 in the TOC (hides H1 and H5+).

#### Per-page — disable TOC entirely

```yaml
---
hide_table_of_contents: true
---
```

#### Inline TOC within the page body

```mdx
import TOCInline from '@theme/TOCInline';

<TOCInline toc={toc} />
```

The `toc` object is available in MDX scope on doc pages.

#### Site-wide default

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):

```js
docs: {
  tocMinHeadingLevel: 2,
  tocMaxHeadingLevel: 4,
},
```

---

### 7. Ability to add/change related articles

**Verdict:** Yes — requires setup (no native "related articles" field).

**Current state:**
- No related-articles section exists on any page.
- Internal links exist inline (e.g. links to help.socprime.com and tdm.socprime.com) but not as a structured "Related" block.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — Manual markdown section (quickest)

Add at the bottom of each MDX file:

```mdx
## Related articles

- [Active Threats](/docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats)
- [Search](/docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/search)
- [Lucene Syntax](https://help.socprime.com/en/articles/6265772-lucene-syntax)
```

Use root-relative paths (`/docs/...`) — Docusaurus resolves them correctly regardless of `baseUrl`.

#### Option B — Frontmatter-driven component (recommended)

1. Frontmatter:

```yaml
---
related:
  - product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats
  - product/threat-detection-marketplace/search
  - faq/email-notifications
---
```

2. Create `src/components/RelatedArticles/index.js`:

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import Link from '@docusaurus/Link';

const LABELS = {
  'product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats': 'Active Threats',
  'product/threat-detection-marketplace/search': 'Search',
  'faq/email-notifications': 'Email Notifications',
};

export default function RelatedArticles({items = []}) {
  if (!items.length) return null;
  return (
    <section className="related-articles">
      <h2>Related articles</h2>
      <ul>
        {items.map((id) => (
          <li key={id}>
            <Link to={`/docs/${id}`}>{LABELS[id] ?? id}</Link>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </section>
  );
}
```

3. Use a docs wrapper or swizzle `DocItem/Content` to auto-render `related` frontmatter.

#### Option C — Tag-based related articles

If tags are enabled (see Requirement 18), the tag page at `/docs/tags/<tag>` automatically lists all docs sharing that tag — a form of automated related content.

---

### 8. Ability to add/change language versions

**Verdict:** Yes — requires setup (currently English only).

**Current state:**

```js
i18n: {
  defaultLocale: 'en',
  locales: ['en'],
},
```

Only one locale is configured. No translated content folders exist.

**How to implement:**

#### Step 1 — Add locales in config

```js
i18n: {
  defaultLocale: 'en',
  locales: ['en', 'uk', 'de'],  // example locales
  localeConfigs: {
    en: { label: 'English', direction: 'ltr' },
    uk: { label: 'Українська', direction: 'ltr' },
    de: { label: 'Deutsch', direction: 'ltr' },
  },
},
```

#### Step 2 — Generate translation templates

```bash
npm run write-translations
```

This creates `i18n/en/code.json` and similar files for UI strings (navbar, footer, etc.).

#### Step 3 — Create translated doc files

For each locale, create mirrored content:

```
docs/
  getting-started.mdx          ← English (default)
i18n/
  uk/
    docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/
      current/
        getting-started.mdx    ← Ukrainian translation
  de/
    docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/
      current/
        getting-started.mdx    ← German translation
```

#### Step 4 — Build and deploy all locales

```bash
npm run build
```

Docusaurus builds all locales. The locale dropdown appears in the navbar automatically.

#### Step 5 — SEO: `hreflang` tags

Docusaurus automatically adds `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="...">` tags when multiple locales are configured.

**Considerations:**
- Each language version needs its own translated MDX file (or at minimum translated frontmatter/titles).
- The help-center import script would need a `--locale` option to import from different Intercom language URLs (e.g. `/en/articles/...` vs `/uk/articles/...`).
- Effort is **high** if translating all existing content; **medium** if only adding the infrastructure and translating incrementally.

---

### 9. Ability to add to subdomain or main domain (`help.socprime.com` / `socprime.com/help`)

**Verdict:** Yes — requires DNS and hosting configuration (not a Docusaurus limitation).

**Current state:**
- Deployed to GitHub Pages at `https://sleepycapibara.github.io/Docu-Docu/`.
- `url` and `baseUrl` in config point to the GitHub Pages project site.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — Subdomain: `help.socprime.com` (recommended for help centers)

1. **Update [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):**

```js
url: 'https://help.socprime.com',
baseUrl: '/',
trailingSlash: false,
```

2. **Add a CNAME file** at [`static/CNAME`](static/CNAME):

```txt
help.socprime.com
```

3. **Configure DNS** (at your domain registrar / Cloudflare):

```
help.socprime.com  CNAME  <your-github-pages-target>
```

   For GitHub Pages custom domains, point to `<user>.github.io` or use A records for apex if needed.

4. **Configure custom domain in GitHub repo settings:** Settings → Pages → Custom domain → `help.socprime.com`.

5. **Enable HTTPS** in GitHub Pages settings (automatic with custom domain).

6. **Update deploy workflow** if switching away from `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages` to another host (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages, S3+CloudFront).

#### Option B — Subpath: `socprime.com/help`

This requires the main `socprime.com` site to **reverse-proxy** `/help` to the Docusaurus deployment.

1. **Docusaurus config:**

```js
url: 'https://socprime.com',
baseUrl: '/help/',
trailingSlash: false,
```

2. **On the main site server** (nginx example):

```nginx
location /help/ {
  proxy_pass https://<docusaurus-host>/;
  proxy_set_header Host $host;
}
```

3. **Challenges:**
   - Asset paths must match `baseUrl` (`/help/`).
   - The main site and docs site must coordinate deployments.
   - Sitemap URL becomes `https://socprime.com/help/sitemap.xml`.
   - More complex than a subdomain; only choose this if brand/URL policy requires it.

#### Option C — Keep GitHub Pages, change only the domain

Same as Option A. The deploy workflow in [`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) does not need changes — only config + DNS + CNAME.

**Current gap:** Production URL is a GitHub Pages dev URL, not `help.socprime.com`. This must be changed before launch.

---

### 10. Ability to add Schemas (FAQ, Author, Type of Page)

**Verdict:** Yes — requires setup (no built-in schema UI; JSON-LD is added manually or via plugin).

**Current state:**
- No structured data (JSON-LD) is present on any page.
- FAQ pages exist but do not use FAQ schema.

**How to implement:**

#### FAQ schema (for FAQ pages)

Add to any FAQ MDX file:

```mdx
<head>
  <script type="application/ld+json">
    {JSON.stringify({
      '@context': 'https://schema.org',
      '@type': 'FAQPage',
      mainEntity: [
        {
          '@type': 'Question',
          name: 'How do I turn on email notifications?',
          acceptedAnswer: {
            '@type': 'Answer',
            text: 'Navigate to Account → Email Notifications tab and enable notifications.',
          },
        },
        {
          '@type': 'Question',
          name: 'How often can I receive notifications?',
          acceptedAnswer: {
            '@type': 'Answer',
            text: 'You can choose every 1, 3, 5, 7, or 30 days.',
          },
        },
      ],
    })}
  </script>
</head>
```

#### Article / TechArticle schema (for product docs)

```mdx
<head>
  <script type="application/ld+json">
    {JSON.stringify({
      '@context': 'https://schema.org',
      '@type': 'TechArticle',
      headline: 'Active Threats',
      description: 'Guide for using Active Threats in Threat Detection Marketplace.',
      author: { '@type': 'Person', name: 'Eugene' },
      datePublished: '2023-02-02',
      dateModified: '2026-05-18',
      publisher: {
        '@type': 'Organization',
        name: 'SOC Prime',
        logo: { '@type': 'ImageObject', url: 'https://help.socprime.com/img/logo.svg' },
      },
    })}
  </script>
</head>
```

#### Author schema

```json
{
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Eugene",
  "worksFor": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SOC Prime"
  }
}
```

Embed as part of the Article schema's `author` field, or as a standalone `ProfilePage`.

#### WebPage / BreadcrumbList schema

Docusaurus breadcrumbs (Requirement 14) do not automatically emit `BreadcrumbList` JSON-LD. Add manually:

```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
  "itemListElement": [
    { "@type": "ListItem", "position": 1, "name": "Docs", "item": "https://help.socprime.com/docs" },
    { "@type": "ListItem", "position": 2, "name": "FAQs", "item": "https://help.socprime.com/docs/faq" },
    { "@type": "ListItem", "position": 3, "name": "Email Notifications" }
  ]
}
```

#### Scalable approach — reusable component

Create `src/components/StructuredData/index.js` that accepts props (`type`, `data`) and renders the `<script type="application/ld+json">` tag. Use frontmatter to pass data:

```yaml
---
schema:
  type: FAQPage
  questions:
    - q: "How do I turn on email notifications?"
      a: "Navigate to Account → Email Notifications tab."
---
```

Then swizzle `DocItem/Content` to inject the component when `schema` frontmatter is present.

#### Plugin alternative

Community plugins like `docusaurus-plugin-seo` or custom remark/rehype plugins can automate schema injection. No plugin is currently installed.

---

### 11. Ability to change/optimize webpage URL structure

**Verdict:** Yes — fully supported.

**Current state:**
- URLs follow the folder structure: `/docs/<section>/<category>/<slug>`.
- Example: `/docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats`.
- `baseUrl` prefix adds `/Docu-Docu/` in the current deployment.

**How to implement:**

#### Per-page slug override

```yaml
---
slug: /active-threats-guide
---
```

This changes the URL to `/docs/active-threats-guide` (replaces the default path derived from the file location).

#### Per-page custom ID (filename ≠ URL)

```yaml
---
id: active-threats
---
```

The file can be named anything; the URL uses `id`.

#### Folder structure controls URL hierarchy

```
docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats.mdx
→ /docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats
```

Reorganize folders to change URLs. Update internal links and add redirects for old URLs.

#### Change the docs base path

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):

```js
docs: {
  routeBasePath: 'help',  // changes /docs/... to /help/...
},
```

#### Trailing slash behavior

```js
trailingSlash: false,  // /docs/faq/email-notifications (no trailing slash)
// or
trailingSlash: true,   // /docs/faq/email-notifications/
```

#### Redirects for old URLs (important during migration from help.socprime.com)

Install the client redirect plugin:

```bash
npm install @docusaurus/plugin-client-redirects
```

```js
plugins: [
  [
    '@docusaurus/plugin-client-redirects',
    {
      redirects: [
        {
          from: '/docs/old-slug',
          to: '/docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats',
        },
      ],
      createRedirects(existingPath) {
        // e.g. strip /product/ prefix for old URLs
      },
    },
  ],
],
```

**Migration note:** Map each `help.socprime.com/en/articles/<id>-<slug>` URL to the new Docusaurus path and create redirects to preserve SEO equity.

---

### 12. Ability to add/change internal linking between pages

**Verdict:** Yes — fully supported.

**Current state:**
- Inline links exist in article bodies (markdown links to other docs and external URLs).
- Footer in [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js) links to key doc pages.
- Homepage feature cards in [`src/components/HomepageFeatures/index.js`](src/components/HomepageFeatures/index.js) link to doc sections.
- `onBrokenLinks: 'throw'` is enabled — broken internal links fail the build (good for SEO hygiene).

**How to implement:**

#### Markdown links (relative — recommended)

```mdx
[Active Threats](../product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats)
[Email Notifications](/docs/faq/email-notifications)
```

#### Docusaurus Link component (for MDX)

```mdx
import Link from '@docusaurus/Link';

<Link to="/docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/search">Search guide</Link>
```

#### Cross-section linking

```mdx
See the [v6.2.0 release notes](/docs/release-notes/v6.2.0) for related changes.
```

#### Automatic linking via tags

Enable tags (Requirement 18) — each tag page lists all related docs.

#### Sidebar and category index pages

Autogenerated sidebars ([`sidebars.js`](sidebars.js)) and `_category_.json` `generated-index` pages provide automatic navigation links within each section.

**Best practice:** Prefer root-relative `/docs/...` paths for internal links. They survive `baseUrl` changes and are validated at build time.

---

### 13. Ability to add/change images/screenshots inside of page

**Verdict:** Yes — fully supported (already in use).

**Current state:**
- Images are stored in `static/img/docs/<section>/<slug>/` (e.g. `static/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png`).
- Referenced in MDX as `![Product screenshot](/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png)`.
- The import script ([`scripts/import-help-articles.js`](scripts/import-help-articles.js)) downloads and localizes images automatically.

**How to implement:**

#### Add an image manually

1. Place the file in `static/img/docs/<section>/<article>/`.
2. Reference in MDX:

```mdx
![Description of what the screenshot shows](/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png)
```

#### Responsive images with HTML

```mdx
<img
  src="/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png"
  alt="Email notification settings page"
  width="800"
  loading="lazy"
/>
```

#### Import images as modules (for bundling/optimization)

```mdx
import Screenshot from '@site/static/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png';

<img src={Screenshot} alt="Email notification settings" />
```

#### Bulk import from help center

```bash
node scripts/import-help-articles.js --slug email-notifications
```

This fetches the article, downloads all images, and writes the MDX file with local image paths.

---

### 14. Ability to add/change breadcrumbs

**Verdict:** Yes — enabled by default in Docusaurus classic theme.

**Current state:**
- Breadcrumbs are **on** by default (no explicit config disables them).
- They appear as: `Product Docs > Threat Detection Marketplace > Active Threats` (derived from folder structure and sidebar).

**How to implement:**

#### Site-wide toggle

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js) under `themeConfig`:

```js
docs: {
  breadcrumbs: true,   // default
},
```

Set to `false` to disable globally.

#### Breadcrumb structure is controlled by:

1. **Folder hierarchy:** `docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/active-threats.mdx` → Product > Threat Detection Marketplace > Active Threats.
2. **Category labels:** [`docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/_category_.json`](docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/_category_.json) `label` field.
3. **Page title:** The `title` frontmatter or `# H1` heading.

#### Customize category label

In `_category_.json`:

```json
{
  "label": "Threat Detection Marketplace",
  "position": 1,
  "link": {
    "type": "generated-index",
    "description": "Guides for using the Threat Detection Marketplace."
  }
}
```

#### Customize page title in breadcrumb

```yaml
---
title: Active Threats Guide
sidebar_label: Active Threats
---
```

`title` is used in the breadcrumb; `sidebar_label` is used in the sidebar only.

#### Custom breadcrumbs via swizzling

If default breadcrumbs are insufficient, swizzle `@theme/DocBreadcrumbs`:

```bash
npm run swizzle @docusaurus/theme-classic DocBreadcrumbs
```

---

## Would be nice to have

### 15. Ability to add/change alts/descriptions in images

**Verdict:** Yes — supported via Markdown alt text and HTML attributes.

**Current state:**
- All imported images use a generic alt text: `![Product screenshot](...)`.
- No `title` attributes or figcaptions are used.

**How to implement:**

#### Descriptive alt text (recommended)

```mdx
![Email notification settings with frequency dropdown and save button](/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png)
```

#### Alt + title attribute

```mdx
<img
  src="/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png"
  alt="Email notification settings page showing frequency options"
  title="Email Notifications — Frequency settings"
  loading="lazy"
/>
```

#### Figure with caption

```mdx
<figure>
  <img
    src="/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/01.png"
    alt="Email notification settings page"
  />
  <figcaption>Email notification settings — adjust frequency and search profile.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

**Action:** Update the import script to extract `alt` text from help-center image metadata (or prompt for alt text in the manifest). Retroactively improve alt text on existing images for accessibility and image SEO.

---

### 16. Ability to add/change main banner of article for social media

**Verdict:** Yes — via frontmatter `image` field.

**Current state:**
- A site-wide social card is set: `image: 'img/socprime-social-card.svg'` in `themeConfig`.
- No per-article social images are configured.

**How to implement:**

#### Per-article Open Graph image

```yaml
---
image: /img/docs/faq/email-notifications/social-card.png
---
```

This sets `og:image` and `twitter:image` for that page. Recommended size: **1200 × 630 px**.

#### Per-article with absolute URL (required by some platforms)

```yaml
---
image: https://help.socprime.com/img/docs/faq/email-notifications/social-card.png
---
```

#### Fallback hierarchy

1. Page `image` frontmatter (highest priority)
2. `themeConfig.image` (site-wide default)
3. No image (platforms pick whatever they find)

**Action:** Create a social card template (branded banner with article title) and add `image` frontmatter to each article. The import script can auto-generate or assign a default social image per section.

---

### 17. Ability to add/change categories to group pages by relevancy

**Verdict:** Yes — already in use.

**Current state:**
- Categories are defined via `_category_.json` files:
  - [`docs/product/_category_.json`](docs/product/_category_.json) — "Product"
  - [`docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/_category_.json`](docs/product/threat-detection-marketplace/_category_.json) — "Threat Detection Marketplace"
  - [`docs/faq/_category_.json`](docs/faq/_category_.json) — "FAQs"
  - [`docs/release-notes/_category_.json`](docs/release-notes/_category_.json) — "Release Notes"
- Each category generates an index page with a description and a card list of child pages.

**How to implement:**

#### Add a new category

1. Create a folder: `docs/product/uncoder-ai/`.
2. Add `_category_.json`:

```json
{
  "label": "Uncoder AI",
  "position": 2,
  "link": {
    "type": "generated-index",
    "description": "Guides for Uncoder AI detection engineering."
  }
}
```

3. Add MDX files inside the folder. Sidebar and breadcrumbs update automatically.

#### Change category label or order

Edit `label` (display name) or `position` (sort order) in `_category_.json`.

#### Collapsible sidebar categories

```json
{
  "label": "Threat Detection Marketplace",
  "position": 1,
  "collapsible": true,
  "collapsed": false
}
```

#### Hide a category from sidebar but keep pages accessible

```json
{
  "label": "Internal",
  "className": "hidden-category"
}
```

Then hide via CSS in `custom.css`.

---

### 18. Ability to add/change tags to make more relevant internal linking

**Verdict:** Yes — requires one-time setup (not yet configured).

**Current state:**
- No tags are defined on any doc page.
- No tag index pages exist.

**How to implement:**

#### Step 1 — Enable tags in config

In [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):

```js
docs: {
  tags: ['tdm', 'uncoder', 'notifications', 'coralogix', 'release'],
},
```

Or allow any tag (no whitelist):

```js
docs: {
  // no tags array = all tags allowed
},
```

#### Step 2 — Add tags to frontmatter

```yaml
---
tags: [tdm, notifications, email]
---
```

#### Step 3 — Tag pages are auto-generated

- All tags: `/docs/tags`
- Specific tag: `/docs/tags/notifications` — lists every doc tagged `notifications`.

#### Step 4 — Use tags for related content

Tag pages serve as automatically maintained "related articles" indexes. Link to them from hub pages:

```mdx
Browse all [notification-related articles](/docs/tags/notifications).
```

#### Step 5 — Hide tags from doc page display (optional)

Tags appear at the top of the doc page by default. To restyle, override `.theme-doc-footer` or swizzle the tags component.

**Recommended tags for this project:** `tdm`, `uncoder-ai`, `detectflow`, `notifications`, `coralogix`, `active-threats`, `search`, `release-notes`, `siem`, `mitre-attack`.

---

### 19. Ability to make visual changes on hub pages for UX

**Verdict:** Yes — requires custom development (hub pages exist but use default Docusaurus styling).

**Current state:**
- Category hub pages use `generated-index` type (auto-generated card grid).
- The homepage ([`src/pages/index.js`](src/pages/index.js)) has custom styling with feature cards ([`src/components/HomepageFeatures`](src/components/HomepageFeatures/index.js)).
- Design system CSS exists at [`src/css/design-system.css`](src/css/design-system.css).

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — Customize generated-index pages via CSS

Target the generated index layout in [`src/css/custom.css`](src/css/custom.css):

```css
.generated-index-page .card {
  border-radius: 12px;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
}
```

#### Option B — Replace generated-index with a custom React page

1. Change `_category_.json`:

```json
{
  "label": "FAQs",
  "link": {
    "type": "doc",
    "id": "faq/index"
  }
}
```

2. Create `docs/faq/index.mdx` with custom content and components.

#### Option C — Custom standalone hub pages

Create pages in `src/pages/`:

```
src/pages/product-hub.js     → /product-hub
src/pages/faq-hub.js         → /faq-hub
```

Use the same card components as the homepage. Link from navbar instead of (or in addition to) the default doc sidebar.

#### Option D — Enrich generated-index with description and icons

```json
{
  "label": "Threat Detection Marketplace",
  "link": {
    "type": "generated-index",
    "title": "Threat Detection Marketplace Guides",
    "description": "Step-by-step guides for discovering threats, searching detections, and managing your security content.",
    "slug": "/tdm-guides",
    "image": "/img/tdm-hub-banner.png"
  }
}
```

#### Option E — Swizzle the category index component

```bash
npm run swizzle @docusaurus/theme-classic DocCategoryGeneratedIndexPage
```

This gives full control over the hub page layout in React.

**Recommended:** Start with Option D (richer category descriptions + images), then Option C for the homepage-style hub if more UX control is needed.

---

### 20. Ability to add analytics to track activity of users on page

**Verdict:** Yes — requires plugin installation (not yet configured).

**Current state:**
- No analytics plugin or tracking script is installed.
- No cookie consent mechanism exists.

**How to implement:**

#### Option A — Google Analytics 4 (recommended if already using Google)

1. Install:

```bash
npm install @docusaurus/plugin-google-gtag
```

2. Add to [`docusaurus.config.js`](docusaurus.config.js):

```js
plugins: [
  [
    '@docusaurus/plugin-google-gtag',
    {
      trackingID: 'G-XXXXXXXXXX',
      anonymizeIP: true,
    },
  ],
],
```

3. Automatically tracks page views on route changes (SPA-aware).

#### Option B — Google Tag Manager

```bash
npm install @docusaurus/plugin-google-tag-manager
```

```js
plugins: [
  [
    '@docusaurus/plugin-google-tag-manager',
    {
      containerID: 'GTM-XXXXXXX',
    },
  ],
],
```

GTM allows managing multiple tracking tags (GA4, Hotjar, LinkedIn Insight, etc.) without code changes.

#### Option C — Plausible (privacy-friendly, no cookie banner)

Add to `headTags` in config:

```js
headTags: [
  {
    tagName: 'script',
    attributes: {
      defer: true,
      'data-domain': 'help.socprime.com',
      src: 'https://plausible.io/js/script.js',
    },
  },
],
```

#### Option D — Custom event tracking

For specific interactions (search, feedback, outbound links), use a custom React component:

```jsx
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useLocation } from '@docusaurus/router';

export function Analytics() {
  const location = useLocation();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (window.gtag) {
      window.gtag('config', 'G-XXXXXXXXXX', { page_path: location.pathname });
    }
  }, [location]);
  return null;
}
```

Swizzle `@theme/Root` to include it site-wide.

#### Privacy / GDPR consideration

If using GA4 or GTM, add a cookie consent banner (e.g. via GTM or a custom component). Plausible does not require consent in most jurisdictions.

---

## Recommended next steps (priority order)

These are the highest-impact actions to close the gap between current state and production-ready SEO:

1. **Configure production domain** — Change `url`/`baseUrl` to `help.socprime.com`, add `static/CNAME`, configure DNS. (Requirement 9)

2. **Add `title` and `description` frontmatter** to all existing MDX articles. Extend the import script to populate them automatically. (Requirement 2)

3. **Replace hardcoded author/date lines** with structured frontmatter (`authors`, `date`, `last_update`) and a display component. (Requirements 4, 5)

4. **Add JSON-LD schema** to FAQ pages (FAQPage) and product docs (TechArticle). Create a reusable `StructuredData` component. (Requirement 10)

5. **Set up redirects** from old `help.socprime.com/en/articles/...` URLs to new paths. (Requirement 11)

6. **Improve image alt text** — Replace generic "Product screenshot" with descriptive alts. Update import script. (Requirement 15)

7. **Add per-article social images** (`image` frontmatter). (Requirement 16)

8. **Enable tags** and tag all existing articles for automatic related-content linking. (Requirements 7, 18)

9. **Add analytics** (GA4 or Plausible). (Requirement 20)

10. **Plan i18n** — Add locale infrastructure when translations are ready. (Requirement 8)

---

## What Docusaurus does NOT provide out of the box

These are not blockers but require custom work:

| Capability | Gap | Workaround |
|------------|-----|------------|
| Original author field | Docs plugin has no `author` field | Custom frontmatter + component |
| Original publication date | Docs plugin has no `date` field | Custom frontmatter + component |
| Related articles block | No native field | Custom frontmatter + component, or tags |
| Structured data / JSON-LD | No auto-generation | Manual JSON-LD or custom plugin |
| Per-page social image | Supported via frontmatter but not set up | Add `image` to frontmatter |
| WYSIWYG / CMS editing | File-based (MDX in Git) | Use Git-based workflow, or integrate a headless CMS (e.g. Netlify CMS, Decap CMS) |
| Subpath hosting (`socprime.com/help`) | Config supports it but needs reverse proxy | Infrastructure task, not Docusaurus |

None of these gaps prevent meeting the requirements. They require one-time setup that this document describes above.

| [`sidebars.js`](sidebars.js) | Sidebar structure (autogenerated per section) |
| [`docs/**/_category_.json`](docs/) | Category labels, hub page config |
| [`docs/**/*.mdx`](docs/) | Article content and frontmatter |
| [`static/img/docs/**`](static/img/docs/) | Localized screenshots |
| [`scripts/import-help-articles.js`](scripts/import-help-articles.js) | Help center import pipeline |

| [`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) | CI/CD deploy to GitHub Pages |
| [`src/pages/index.js`](src/pages/index.js) | Custom homepage |
| [`src/css/custom.css`](src/css/custom.css) | Theme overrides |
